---
output: github_document 
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Please edit that file  -->

<p align="center">
  <img src="images/R Programming.png" alt="R Programming" width="600">
</p>


```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/README-",
  out.width = "100%"
)
```

# aitools <img src="man/figures/logo.png" align="right" height="138" alt="aitools package" />

<!-- badges: start -->
<!-- badges: end -->

This R Package contains a collection of functions to access AI models

take a look at my youtube playlist with R Tutorials: 

---

https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLRbCt61PaxX2d0_QXh6Qi6_jAQd66fmcI

---

## Installation

You can install the development version of aitools from [GitHub](https://github.com/) with:

``` r
# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("EduardoJacob/aitools")
```

### Defined Functions


### `gemini(prompt, model="2.5-flash", image)`

This function submits a text prompt (and optionally an image) to the Google Gemini API. It requires a `GEMINI_API_KEY` to be set in the `.Renviron` file and uses the `gemini.R` package internally to handle the request. The function tracks and reports the execution time for each call.

### `lmstudio(prompt, model, image)`

This function provides an interface to local models running via LM Studio. If no model is specified, it automatically selects the first available model from the LM Studio instance. It supports both direct text/image prompts and an interactive mode using a `shinychat` app if the prompt argument is omitted.

### `ollama(prompt)`

This function interacts with locally hosted models through Ollama. It automatically identifies the most recently listed model on the system and uses the `ellmer` package to initiate a chat session. Similar to other functions in this set, it supports both single-prompt execution and an interactive chat interface.

### `openrouter(model="openrouter/auto", prompt)`

This function serves as a wrapper for the OpenRouter API, allowing access to a wide variety of models through a single interface using the `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` environment variable. It defaults to the 'auto' model selection and supports launching an interactive `shinychat` session when called without a specific prompt.
